Splash Training Guide

CFB Confidence Pick’em

A private, invite-only guide to running and playing a College Football Confidence Pick’em contest on Splash.

Contents

1What Is CFB Confidence Pick’em?2Setting Up a Contest3My Entries & Making Picks4Contest Settings — General5Contest Settings — Format & Spreads6Contest Settings — Scoring7Contest Settings — Picksheet8Contest Settings — Segments9Contest Settings — Tiebreakers10Contest Settings — Design11Entrants Manager & Invites Manager12Leaderboards13Missed Picks & Scoring Quick Reference14FAQ15Glossary
1 Overview

What Is CFB Confidence Pick’em?

CFB Confidence Pick’em is a private, invite-only, multi-week contest built on top of Splash’s Football Pick’em product. Every week, entrants pick winners for that week’s college football slate — and totals (over/unders), if the commissioner has turned those on — and then rank every one of their picks from most to least confident. Correct picks score the point value tied to their rank, so the pick an entrant is surest about carries the most points, and the pick they’re least sure about carries the fewest.

This guide covers the invite-only, College Football (CFB), Confidence-format version of the product specifically. The three building blocks a commissioner chooses when setting up any Splash Pick’em contest are:

ChoiceWhat this contest usesWhat it means
League / Sport ModeCollege (CFB) onlyThe picksheet draws only from college football conferences and the AP Top 25 — no NFL games.
Format (scoring style)ConfidenceEntrants rank every pick; a correct pick earns its assigned rank value instead of a flat 1 point.
Point Spread ModeStraight Up, or Against the Spread (Splash-set or Commissioner-set)Straight Up only cares who wins outright. Against the Spread requires the picked team to cover a point spread.

On top of those three choices, the commissioner can layer in Segments (mini-leaderboards for a range of weeks), Tiebreakers, a Drop Worst Week rule, and a custom banner — all covered in their own sections below.

Note: Confidence and the Best Pick bonus feature (a Standard-format add-on) are mutually exclusive. A contest runs one or the other, never both — so you won’t see Best Pick anywhere in a Confidence contest.

Quick facts

  • Invite-only — entrants join by invitation, not by open sign-up.
  • One entrant can hold up to 250 entries in a single contest if the commissioner raises the cap (the default is 1).
  • Runs a full college football season: Week 1 through the National Championship, including bowl season.
  • Nearly every commissioner setting can be changed mid-season, with clear rules for what happens to weeks that have already been graded.
Quick Check

In Confidence scoring, a correctly-picked game ranked #1 (least confident) earns:

True or false: entrants in this format only need to pick winners — no confidence ranking is required.

↑ Back to top
2 Setup

Setting Up a Contest

A commissioner sets up a CFB Confidence contest once, then can revisit any setting throughout the season. All contest configuration lives under Contest Settings, reached from the commissioner-only section of the left navigation.

The panel includes a “Search by setting or key” box at the top so a commissioner (or a support agent walking them through it) can jump straight to a setting by name, plus a Back to contest detail link to return to the main contest view. Each tab in the list also has an info icon with a short description of what it controls. The seven tabs, in order, are:

  1. General — contest name, schedule, entry controls, and cancellation.
  2. Format & Spreads — scoring mode and confidence value configuration.
  3. Scoring — drop-worst-week rules.
  4. Picksheet — pick deadlines, autopick, and which games/conferences appear each week.
  5. Segments — optional mini-leaderboards for a range of weeks.
  6. Tiebreakers — how ties are broken on the weekly and season leaderboards.
  7. Design — a custom banner image for the contest page.

From the Dashboard, a commissioner also sees live counters for entries available, invites sent, and how many entrants have joined, alongside a 4-step onboarding checklist: contest created → set up entry fee collection (Contest Vault) → review contest settings → send invites.

Note: Because this format is invite-only, the Contest Vault step is about optional fee collection for the group, not a requirement to run the contest — there’s no entry-fee mechanic built into Pick’em itself.
Quick Check

Which Contest Settings tab controls how many confidence values are available in a given week?

Where does a commissioner go to quickly jump to a specific setting by name?

↑ Back to top
3 Gameplay

My Entries & Making Picks

The left navigation is split into two groups. Everyone sees the player group: Contest details, My entries, and Activity. Commissioners additionally see the commissioner group: Dashboard, Contest settings, Entrants manager, Invites manager, and Contest Vault. Above both groups sits an entries widget showing the lock countdown, total entries, and fill progress toward the contest max.

My entries

Each entry card shows a status indicator, a pencil icon to rename the entry, a Withdraw option, and a Make picks button.

Warn: Withdrawing an entry is permanent and irreversible. Splash confirms with a modal warning that this action cannot be undone before it goes through.

Making picks

Inside Make picks, week and slate tabs sit across the top. For each game, the entrant selects a winner (and an over/under total, if the commissioner has enabled totals) and then assigns a confidence value to that pick from the week’s available range — the value the commissioner configured under Format & Spreads. A progress bar at the bottom tracks how many picks have been made (e.g. “12/16 picks made”).

Warn: Nothing is registered until the entrant clicks Save picks. Picks can be made in advance and revised as many times as needed right up until that week’s pick deadline — but only what’s saved counts.

Activity tab

Two sub-tabs live here: Activity, a chronological log of commissioner setting changes and group membership moves, and Pick changes, a subset showing only instances where the commissioner made a pick on an entrant’s behalf. Pick changes is only relevant if Hide Future Picks From Commissioner (a General setting) is turned off. Both sub-tabs start empty until changes actually occur.

Quick Check

What happens if an entrant fills out all their picks for the week but never clicks Save picks?

Can a withdrawn entry be restored?

↑ Back to top
4 Settings

Contest Settings — General

The General tab covers the basic identity, schedule, and entry rules for the contest.

General settings

Contest name is a free-text field (shown here as “Abbey’s Confidence Test”), and Description is an optional free-text field capped at 500 characters. Both are editable at any time.

Set your schedule

Start week and End week define the first and last week of the contest, each shown with its real calendar date range underneath (e.g. Week 1 runs Aug 31–Sep 7, 2026; CFB National Championship runs Jan 24–31, 2027). The end week can be set anywhere from Week 1 through the National Championship, as long as it’s on or after the start week.

Warn: Moving the start or end week to exclude a week that already has picks wipes those picks — treat this as a destructive action once the season is underway.

Entry controls

SettingWhat it doesNotes
Maximum entries per entrantCaps how many entries one person can hold (1–250)Defaults to 1. Can be raised any time, but never lowered once the contest is live.
Entry deadlineCutoff date/time after which no new entries are acceptedOff by default. Toggle on to pick a custom date and time.
Lock entries immediatelyStops new members from joining right away, regardless of the entry deadlineA hard, immediate stop — independent of whatever the entry deadline says.

Hide future picks & danger zone

Hide Future Picks From Commissioner sets whether the commissioner can see and edit members’ picks before they lock. Turning this on is what makes the Pick changes sub-tab (see the previous section) meaningful — if it’s off, the commissioner can see and adjust picks in advance.

Warn: The Danger zone’s Cancel contest button permanently closes the contest. This can’t be undone.
Tip: Don’t forget to hit Save changes in the sticky footer after editing any General setting.
Quick Check

What's the default for Maximum entries per entrant, and can it be changed later?

If Hide Future Picks From Commissioner is turned ON, what does that mean for the commissioner?

↑ Back to top
5 Settings

Contest Settings — Format & Spreads

This tab sets how picks are scored and, for Confidence contests specifically, how confidence values behave.

Scoring mode

ModeHow it's gradedWho sets the number
Straight upThe picked team just needs to win the game outright.N/A — no spread involved, though spreads can still be shown for informational context.
Against the spread — Splash-setThe picked team must cover a live, dynamically-updating spread.Splash’s odds, which update through the week and then freeze at the spread lock time.
Against the spread — Commissioner-setSame covering logic as above.The commissioner manually enters a spread for every game.

Display spreads on pick sheet shows point spreads for informational purposes only when Straight Up is selected — it doesn’t affect scoring.

Confidence values

This part of the tab configures confidence behavior for missed picks and playoff slates.

Missed pick confidence order decides which values get burned first when an entrant misses picks for the week:

OptionWhat happens on a missed pick
High to lowMissed picks consume the highest remaining confidence values first — a costlier penalty.
Low to highMissed picks consume the lowest remaining confidence values first — a gentler penalty.
Note: This example contest has High to low selected. Either option is valid — it’s a commissioner preference, and it’s worth confirming which one a given contest is using before answering a player’s question about a missed-pick score.

Confidence values per week lists every week and bowl slate as an expandable row, showing how many values are available and confirming that Rank 1 is always the highest confidence. By default, the number of values matches that week’s game count.

Values continue past the regular season into the bowl structure — for example, this contest carries CFB Bowls Week 1 + Army v. Navy (4 values), Week 2 (11), Week 3 (14), Week 4 (13), CFB Semi-Finals (2), and CFB National Championship (1 value).

Tip: Commissioners can hover over any rank and use the up/down arrows to edit that value directly — and, notably, the same number can be reused across multiple ranks if the commissioner wants to. Values don’t have to be unique per rank.
Quick Check

If Missed pick confidence order is set to High to low, what happens when an entrant misses picks?

How many confidence values are available for the CFB National Championship in this contest?

↑ Back to top
6 Settings

Contest Settings — Scoring

The Scoring tab is where a commissioner sets up a Drop worst week rule — a way to forgive one or more bad weeks over the course of the season.

With the toggle on, two dropdowns appear:

SettingWhat it does
Weeks to dropHow many of an entry's lowest-scoring weeks get automatically excluded from their season total (e.g. “1 lowest-scoring week”).
Last drop weekThe final week still eligible to be dropped (e.g. “No limit” allows any week in the contest to count).

An inline confirmation banner restates the current configuration in plain language: “Each entry’s 1 lowest-scoring week will be automatically excluded from their total.”

Note: Drop worst week only factors into the standings once an entry has completed more weeks than the number set to drop — it won’t apply prematurely early in the season.
Tip: As with every settings tab, click Save changes after adjusting Weeks to drop or Last drop week.
Quick Check

What does 'Last drop week: No limit' mean?

↑ Back to top
7 Settings

Contest Settings — Picksheet

Picksheet is the largest settings tab: pick lock rules, autopick behavior, and exactly which games and conferences show up on the weekly picksheet.

Pick requirements

Pick deadline sets when the weekly picksheet locks: the last game of the week, the first game of the week, a default of Saturdays at 11:00 AM CDT, or a fully custom day/time.

Picks per week is either Pick all games, or a custom number the commissioner types in — a week with fewer games than that custom number automatically falls back to pick-all.

Autopick

Autopick automatically fills in picks for players who miss the weekly deadline.

Who sets the auto-pick setting? is either Commissioner sets for all (one logic applies to every entrant) or Each player chooses (entrants pick their own auto-pick preference when they join, and can update it later from their entry settings).

FieldOptionsWhat it does
Team pick logicAll home teams, All away teams, All favorites, All underdogs, RandomFills in a missed team pick automatically, following this rule.
Over/Under logicAll overs, All unders, RandomFills in a missed totals pick automatically, following this rule (independent of team pick logic).
Maximum amount of auto picksOff, or a Maximum count with a Forgiveness per week valueCaps how many times a player can receive auto-picks over the full contest. Once the cap is hit, further missed picks are simply scored as losses.
Note: Forgiveness per week is the number of games an entrant picks themselves in a given week to avoid that week counting against their autopick cap at all — it only appears once Maximum amount of auto picks is turned on.

Picksheet Setup

Picksheet Setup has two sub-tabs: Season defaults, which apply all season unless overridden, and Weekly customizations, which override those defaults for one specific week.

The Conferences & Over/Under totals table lists every eligible league/conference — SEC, Big Ten, ACC, Big 12, Pac-12, American, Mountain West, Sun Belt, MAC, Conference USA, FBS Independents, and AP Top 25 (tagged “RANKED”) — each with its own checkbox to include it on the picksheet, plus a separate Totals checkbox to turn on over/under picks for that conference independently. A “Select all” shortcut and a running count (e.g. “12 of 12 selected”) sit above the table.

Below that, Eligible game days is a Mon–Sun multi-select controlling which days of the week can have games appear on the picksheet at all.

Weekly customizations lets the commissioner step through each week (via the prev/next arrows or the week dropdown) and override the defaults just for that week: a league pill bar to filter the view, an Include Over/Under Totals toggle with Select all/Clear all shortcuts, and a per-game table grouped by date. Each game has its own include checkbox and a separate Must pick checkbox, with a matching pair of checkboxes for that game’s Over/Under Total row.

Warn: Unchecking a game that already has picks on it wipes those picks — and if a game’s winner pick is disabled, its Over/Under pick must be disabled too.

Bowl Mode

Bowl Mode combines all bowl games into a single picksheet and automatically concludes the contest once the CFB season ends, right after the National Championship. It isn’t available yet in a season that hasn’t reached bowl selection — the card stays locked until roughly bowl selection Sunday in early December.

Warn: Once Bowl Mode is turned on, it cannot be turned back off. Enabling it also resets that slate’s game weights and Must Picks, and re-ranks confidence values to match the bowl slate’s game count.
Quick Check

If 'Each player chooses' is selected for the auto-pick setting, when do players set their preference?

What happens once Bowl Mode is turned on?

On the Weekly customizations view, if a game's winner pick is unchecked, what else must happen?

↑ Back to top
8 Settings

Contest Settings — Segments

Segments let a commissioner group weeks into named, mini leaderboards — useful for awarding a prize halfway through the season, or just giving players something to compete for besides the full-season standings.

Each segment group has an editable name (e.g. “First Quarter”), a trash icon to delete it, and Start week / End week dropdowns defining which weeks feed into that segment’s leaderboard. Clicking Add Segment creates another one.

Tip: Segments can be added or deleted at any time — including retroactively for weeks that have already been played — so a commissioner isn’t locked into deciding on segments before the season starts.
Quick Check

What do Segments create?

↑ Back to top
9 Settings

Contest Settings — Tiebreakers

Tiebreakers set both the tiebreaker game mechanic and the ordered criteria used to rank the weekly and season leaderboards when entrants are tied on points.

Note: Separately from the leaderboard cascades below, a commissioner can turn on a Weekly Tiebreaker: a predicted-score guess on a designated game (by default, the last game on that week’s picksheet). A missed guess defaults to 0. This is what powers the Tie Game Points and Tie Game Pick criteria in the cascades — without it turned on, those two options won’t be available to add.

Both the Weekly Leaderboard Tiebreakers and Season Leaderboard Tiebreakers cascades work the same way: an ordered list where the first item is the highest priority, each row has up/down arrows to reorder it and an X to remove it, and an ADD row of pill buttons to bring in more criteria.

CriterionNotes
PointsAlways active and can never be removed — every row above it is settled first, and Points is the ultimate fallback.
WinsIncluded by default in this contest's cascade.
Tie Game PointsIncluded by default; requires Weekly Tiebreaker to be turned on.
+ # of Auto Picks UsedAvailable to add; lower auto-pick usage wins the tiebreak.
+ Win %Available to add.
+ Win MarginAvailable to add; Over/Under picks factor in here too (e.g. picking the over on a 42-point total that lands at 52 adds +10 to Win Margin).
+ Tie Game PickAvailable to add; requires Weekly Tiebreaker to be turned on.
Tip: Both cascades are editable at any time, even mid-contest — a commissioner isn’t locked into the order they picked at setup.
Note: Because this is a Confidence-format contest, you won’t see Best Pick Wins as an available add-on — that criterion only applies to Standard-format contests running the Best Pick bonus feature.
Quick Check

Which tiebreaker criterion can never be removed from either cascade?

What has to be turned on before a commissioner can add Tie Game Points or Tie Game Pick to a cascade?

↑ Back to top
10 Settings

Contest Settings — Design

Design is the simplest tab: it controls the custom hero image shown on the contest page.

Clicking Upload banner lets the commissioner choose a PNG or JPG file up to 4 MB to use as the contest’s banner image.

Tip: A custom banner is a nice touch for a private group contest — a team logo, a league mascot, or anything that makes the contest page feel like theirs.
Quick Check

What file types and size limit does the banner upload accept?

↑ Back to top
11 Management

Entrants Manager & Invites Manager

Entrants Manager

Entrants Manager has three items in its left sub-nav: Entrants, Email entrants, and Pick log.

Sub-tabWhat it shows
EntrantsFilter pills (All / Active / Missing picks / Removed / Paused / Notes / No notes) over a searchable table of entrant, name, email, entered date, entries, missing picks, and notes, with a Download button top-right to export the roster.
Email entrantsSend messages directly to some or all entrants.
Pick logEach entrant's already-locked picks by week and slate only — never live or future picks.
Note: Pick log exists specifically as an audit trail — it lets a commissioner settle a “did they actually submit a pick” dispute without ever exposing anyone’s in-progress picks early. Whether the commissioner can also see future picks live (outside the Pick log) depends on the Hide Future Picks From Commissioner setting under General.

Invites Manager

Invites Manager has two tabs: New invite and Sent.

  • New invite offers four ways to add people: Share link, Previous Splash entrants, Followers, and Import emails — plus an “Invite (N)” panel with an optional custom message and a Send invite button.
  • Sent tracks everyone who's already been invited.
Quick Check

Which Entrants Manager sub-tab shows an entrant's already-locked picks, and only by week/slate?

Why does Pick log never show live or future picks?

↑ Back to top
12 Results

Leaderboards

Leaderboards exist at three levels in every Splash Pick’em contest:

  1. Season-long — cumulative standings across every counted week.
  2. Weekly — standings for a single week only.
  3. Picks by week — a grid view showing each entrant's picks week over week.

On top of those three, the settings covered earlier in this guide layer in optional sub-views:

LayerWhere it's configuredWhat it adds
SegmentsContest Settings → SegmentsA separate mini-leaderboard for a defined range of weeks (e.g. “First Quarter”).
Tiebreaker cascadeContest Settings → TiebreakersThe order in which ties are broken on the season and weekly leaderboards.
Drop Worst WeekContest Settings → ScoringExcludes each entry's worst week(s) from their season total before ranking.
Note: Splash also supports optional Groups — team-vs-team standings that combine every member's stats — as a separate layer a commissioner can enable. This particular contest doesn’t have a Groups tab configured, so it isn’t covered in this guide.
Quick Check

Which setting determines the order used to break ties on the season leaderboard?

↑ Back to top
13 Reference

Missed Picks & Scoring Quick Reference

A fast reference for “what happens if…” questions about missed picks and scoring in this contest.

ScenarioWhat happens
Entrant misses the pick deadline, Autopick is onMissed picks are filled automatically, using whichever Team pick logic and Over/Under logic the commissioner (or the entrant, if player-choice is on) selected under Picksheet → Autopick.
Entrant misses the deadline, Autopick is off (or their cap is hit)The missed picks are simply scored as losses.
A missed pick needs a confidence value assignedIt consumes the entrant's remaining confidence values in the order set under Format & Spreads — High to low or Low to high.
An entrant has one unusually bad weekIf Drop Worst Week is on, that week (or up to however many weeks the commissioner allows) is automatically excluded from their season total, as long as they've completed more weeks than the number set to drop.
An entrant joins mid-seasonThey're scored 0-for-every-required-pick on any week that already completed before they joined, and compete normally starting with the next open week.
Two entrants are tied at the end of a week or the seasonTies are broken using the ordered Weekly or Season Leaderboard Tiebreaker cascade set under Tiebreakers, with Points as the unremovable final fallback.
Tip: When in doubt about how a specific missed pick was scored, the Entrants Manager → Pick log is the audit trail to check — it shows exactly what locked in for that entrant, week by week.
Quick Check

If Autopick is off and an entrant misses the pick deadline, what happens to their unmade picks?

What determines which confidence value a missed pick consumes?

↑ Back to top
14 Help

FAQ

Q: Can I change my picks after I've saved them?

Yes — picks can be revised as many times as needed right up until that week's pick deadline. Just remember to click Save picks again after any change.

Q: Can I lower Maximum entries per entrant once the contest is live?

No. It can be raised at any time, but never lowered once the contest has started.

Q: Can Bowl Mode be turned off once it's enabled?

No — Bowl Mode is permanent once turned on. It also can't be enabled at all until later in the season, around bowl selection Sunday in early December.

Q: What happens if I join the contest after it's already started?

A late joiner is scored 0-for-every-required-pick on every week that already completed before they joined, and then competes normally starting with the next open week. Joining isn't possible at all once the contest has ended.

Q: Does changing the Missed pick confidence order affect weeks that already happened?

This setting locks during active weekly grading and reopens once that week concludes, so it's meant to be changed between weeks, not mid-grade.

Q: Can two different picks in the same week share the same confidence value?

By default confidence values are typically unique per rank, but a commissioner can edit individual rank values under Format & Spreads and reuse the same number across multiple ranks if they choose to.

Quick Check

Once Bowl Mode is enabled, can a commissioner turn it back off?

↑ Back to top
15 Reference

Glossary

TermDefinition
Confidence value / rankThe point value an entrant assigns to a single pick each week. Rank 1 is the highest confidence and carries the highest value; a correct pick scores exactly its assigned value.
PicksheetThe weekly list of eligible games (and totals, if enabled) that entrants make picks against.
AutopickA setting that automatically fills in picks for an entrant who misses the weekly deadline, following whatever Team pick logic and Over/Under logic is configured.
Drop Worst WeekA scoring rule that automatically excludes an entry's lowest-scoring week(s) from their season total.
SegmentA named mini-leaderboard covering a defined range of weeks, separate from the full-season standings.
Tiebreaker cascadeThe ordered list of criteria (Points, Wins, Tie Game Points, and so on) used to break ties on a leaderboard.
Weekly TiebreakerAn optional predicted-score guess on a designated game, used to power the Tie Game Points and Tie Game Pick tiebreaker criteria.
Bowl ModeA setting that combines all bowl games into a single picksheet and permanently concludes the contest after the National Championship.
Against the Spread (ATS)A scoring mode where the picked team must cover a point spread, not just win outright.
EntrantA player who has joined the contest, potentially holding one or more entries.
CommissionerThe person who creates and configures the contest.
Quick Check

Which term describes an ordered list of criteria used to break ties on a leaderboard?
